title: EWS can't seek age relaxation, enhanced attempts like SC,ST, OBC: Delhi High Court
content: The Bench observed that the deprivation faced by EWS individuals was not comparable to caste-based discrimination, and the policy not to extend age and attempt relaxation to the EWS category was not "mala fide, arbitrary or unconstitutional" merely because it accorded different relaxations to different reserved categories.

content: As part of containment efforts, 7,444 birds were culled, and over 14,000 eggs and 2,250 kg of poultry feed were scientifically disposed of on April 15 under the supervision of the Animal Husbandry Department.

title: With BDS student’s death in Kannur, caste bias back in focus
content: The death of a student of Kannur Dental College has raised disturbing questions about caste discrimination on Kerala’s college campuses. The college has dismissed a faculty member and suspended another, pending inquiry. The incident led to an outpouring of related complaints from other students. The bereaved family, the student’s college-mates, faculty, and activists express their views

title: Focus Tamil Nadu | Delimitation Bill sparks fears in Tamil Nadu: what’s at stake?
content: Tamil Nadu is facing a crucial political question that could reshape its voice in Parliament and impact South India as a whole. The Centre’s push for delimitation and a constitutional amendment, linked to women’s reservation, has raised deeper concerns in the state. At the heart of the debate — will Tamil Nadu lose its share in Parliament?

title: More women in the fray, but Tamil Nadu polls remain overwhelmingly male

title: Living with the Mughals: a legacy that endures despite erasure
content: April 21, 2026 marks five centuries since the decisive Battle of Panipat which led to the founding of the Mughal empire in the Indian subcontinent. Memory, monument and everyday language carry forward a contested inheritance that refuses to fade

title: Stalin stands exposed after PM Modi’s assurance on delimitation: Edappadi Palaniswami
content: Pointing out that the share of Tamil Nadu in the Lok Sabha would go up from 7.18% to 7.23%, Mr. Palaniswami, in an interview with The Hindu, said that “this disproves what he [Mr. Stalin] has been saying.”

title: Andhra Pradesh land allotment committee clears 599 proposals worth ₹2037 crore across 19 districts
content: Andhra Pradesh State Land Allotment Committee approved 599 applications worth ₹2,037 crore covering 215.80 acres across 19 districts

title: Blistering heat, soaring UV levels and high humidity keep Kerala on its toes
content: IMD warns of above-average temperatures across State as sweltering, sultry conditions prevail; several places record alarming levels of UV radiation; urban heat island effect amplifies the conditions

title: Man who impersonated IAS officer arrested for ₹41 lakh fraud in Bengaluru
content: According to the complainant, who owns a medical store, the accused was introduced to him through a District Surgeon, and had promised to secure a medicine supply order worth ₹1.8 crore for Primary Health Centres.

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: How a stool at polling booths makes a huge difference for these people
content: Members of Tamil Nadu Dwarfism Association have reiterated a set a demands they have always made when the hustings is round the corner

title: Gukesh, Vaishali World Chess C'ship matches to be held together? Here’s what we know
content: India is set to witness a historic chess moment as young prodigies Dommaraju Gukesh and Vaishali Rameshbabu vie for World Championship titles. Gukesh is the reigning champion, while Vaishali faces the formidable Ju Wenjun. FIDE is now seeking bids for host cities, with a potential for both matches to be held concurrently, creating an unprecedented dual championship event.

title: Microsoft’s & Apple’s coding tools will be ‘dead soon,': Creator of Antrophic's AI coding tool
content: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, predicts traditional coding tools like VS Code and Xcode will soon be obsolete. He believes AI agents will soon handle entire development workflows, rendering current IDEs unnecessary. This shift will redefine software engineers into 'builders' who orchestrate AI, requiring adaptability and early experimentation from professionals.

title: 'Centre trying to bypass OBCs and Dalits’: What Rahul Gandhi said on Delimitation Bill - Top quotes
content: Rahul Gandhi attacked the Women's Reservation Bill, calling it a political maneuver to alter India's electoral map rather than empower women. He alleged the government aims to weaken the political voice of OBCs and Dalits through constituency restructuring, vowing opposition to this "anti-national" move.

title: TCS Nashik case: Hunt on for absconding female employee; cops reach Mumbai, husband questioned
content: Nashik Crime Branch officers are actively searching for a female employee, an accused in a TCS harassment and alleged religious conversion case, who has been absconding and residing in Mumbra for two months. Her husband is currently in police custody and undergoing questioning as the investigation intensifies. This developing story highlights the ongoing efforts to apprehend the suspect.

title: Jharkhand JAC Class 9 result 2026 released: Direct link to download scorecards
content: The Jharkhand Academic Council has declared the JAC Class 9 result 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 99.17%. Over 4.06 lakh students cleared the exam, with girls slightly outperforming boys. Most districts recorded above 98% results, reflecting strong performance. Students can check scores online or via SMS using their roll details.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 30: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2's collection
content: Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi, and Sara Arjun, has made box office history by crossing Rs. 1,100 crore in India net collections, the first Hindi film ever to achieve this milestone. On Day 30, the film's cumulative India net stands at Rs. 1,103.51 crore, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,737.74 crore across 4,64,059 shows globally. Week 1 delivered a record Rs. 674.17 crore, including a Rs. 90 crore opening day, the biggest in Hindi film history. Stay updated with live Day 30 tracking, all weekly breakdowns, and the complete story of the film that rewrote Hindi cinema's box office rulebook.

title: Father of AI Yann LeCun: EU politics is very different from US politics
content: AI pioneer Yann LeCun highlighted significant differences between European and American politics, particularly concerning privacy and government trust. He argued that the EU's strong privacy laws, unlike those in the US, protect citizens from both corporate and governmental intrusion. LeCun also suggested Europeans generally exhibit higher trust in their governments and expect more robust protections.

title: 'Currently weeping': Alia Bhatt breaks down in tears at sister Shaheen Bhatt's engagement
content: Alia Bhatt is thrilled by sister Shaheen Bhatt's engagement to ex-swimmer Ishaan Mehra, shared via Instagram proposal pic: “We may have overdone this whole liking each other thing.” Alia reposted tearfully, “Currently weeping.” Celebs like Soni Razdan, Neetu Kapoor showered love. Couple went official in 2025.

title: Gujarat Titans rope in South African sensation as Tom Banton replacement
content: Gujarat Titans have signed South African wicketkeeper-batter Connor Esterhuizen as a replacement for the injured Tom Banton. Esterhuizen impressed in his recent T20I debut series, earning Player of the Series honours. The young talent, acquired at his base price, adds depth to the Titans' squad as they aim to build momentum in the IPL 2026 season.

title: IPL 2026: RR manager Romi Bhinder 'warned and fined' for using phone in dugout
content: Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder has been let off with just a fine and warning for using mobile phone in the dugout. It can be confirmed that the BCCI ACSU decision has been communicated to the franchise.

title: Patralekha slams body-shaming over post-pregnancy comments
content: Actress Patralekhaa, who recently welcomed her daughter, faced body-shaming online after a film screening. She candidly addressed the insensitive comments on Instagram, highlighting her postpartum reality and the challenges of producing films alongside motherhood.  The actress emphasized the need for kindness and understanding during this significant life phase.

title: 'Not just athleticism, but awareness': Sachin applauds Shreyas Iyer's boundary catch
content: Cricket legend Sachin Tendulkar lauded Shreyas Iyer for a remarkable boundary catch in an IPL 2026 match. Iyer's athletic and intelligent effort saved a boundary and led to a dismissal. Tendulkar highlighted Iyer's exceptional awareness, timing, fitness, and composure in executing the difficult play. The incident impressed players and fans alike.

title: Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni, 16 others gets life imprisonment in 2016 BJP worker murder case
content: A Special Court has sentenced Congress MLA Vinay Kulkarni and 16 others to life imprisonment for the 2016 murder of BJP leader Yogeshgouda Goudar. The court found them guilty of murder and criminal conspiracy. This conviction leads to Kulkarni's immediate disqualification from the Karnataka Legislative Assembly.

title: Nashik TCS case: Absconding female accused to file for anticipatory bail, cites pregnancy
content: Accused in the TCS Nashik case are moving court for bail. Lawyers are preparing applications for both arrested and absconding individuals. One accused seeks anticipatory bail citing pregnancy. Her lawyer states the FIR does not mention forced religious conversion but hurting religious sentiments. Police investigations are ongoing with multiple FIRs filed.

title: Watch: How is the US Navy enforcing blockade amid Hormuz deadlock
content: US Navy forces, aboard the USS Michael Murphy, diverted a merchant vessel in the Gulf of Oman to enforce restrictions on Iran's maritime routes. This operation, involving over 10,000 personnel and multiple naval assets, aims to sustain maritime enforcement in regional waters.

title: 'Since no one is complementing ... ': Shreyas Iyer's cheeky take after breathtaking Hardik Pandya catch - WATCH
content: Shreyas Iyer's spectacular mid-air relay catch wowed fans and drew viral reactions from teammates. Despite a strong century from Quinton de Kock, Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh's consecutive half-centuries powered Punjab Kings to a seven-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, propelling PBKS to the top of the table.

title: Rupee hits one-week high as RBI moves to stem in fall
content: The Indian rupee surged to a one-week high Friday, boosted by Reserve Bank of India's strategic moves to curb dollar demand from state-run oil refiners. By urging them to use a special credit line, the central bank aims to stabilize the currency against global pressures.

title: Former Chicago Bears wide receiver Alshon Jeffery booked in jail for insurance fraud
content: Former Chicago Bears and Philadelphia Eagles star wide receiver Alshon Jeffery was recently arrested for insurance fraud, for allegedly hiding financial information. The Super Bowl champion, who played nine NFL seasons, was booked in jail and later released. California law defines insurance fraud as a felony, potentially carrying five years in prison and/or fine. Details of the alleged fraud remain scarce. He is one of the Greatest Bears of All Time. He won a Super Bowl in 2018 after defeating Tom Brady and Bill Belichick’s team, the New England Patriots.

title: IPL 2026: Amid Sooryavanshi and Arya noise, Prabhsimran Singh is quietly 'raising the bar'
content: Prabhsimran Singh has been part of Punjab Kings, earlier Kings XI Punjab, since 2019, when the IPL franchise splurged Rs 4.8 crore on the youngster. Over the last few seasons, the hard-hitting batter has found consistency, with Ricky Ponting playing a big role in it.

title: Passport row: SC refuses relief to Pawan Khera, declines plea to extend transit bail
content: The Supreme Court denied Congress leader Pawan Khera's request for extended transit anticipatory bail, impacting his efforts to secure protection before an Assam court. This follows the apex court's stay on a Telangana High Court order, raising concerns about "forum shopping" as the FIR originated in Assam. Khera can still seek bail in Assam.

title: Gold enters ‘healthy consolidation phase’ after retail buying surge: Report
content: Gold is experiencing a healthy consolidation after a retail-driven buying spree, particularly in India, China, and the US, according to Jefferies. While demand has cooled, evidenced by a sharp drop in Indian gold imports, the mining sector shows robust fundamentals. Companies are prioritizing shareholder returns over expansion, with strong cash flows expected, suggesting continued sector support.

title: 'Gift wrapped in barbed wire': Tharoor slams Centre for linking women’s quota with Delimitation Bill
content: Senior Congress leader Shashi Tharoor criticized the proposed delimitation exercise as "political demonetisation," arguing that linking women's reservation to constituency redrawing and census data unnecessarily complicates a widely supported reform. He urged immediate implementation of women's reservation, warning that delimitation could disrupt federalism and political power balances.

title: 'False information': Vinod Kambli's wife Andrea Hewitt hits back at health rumours
content: Reports about Vinod Kambli's deteriorating health, including claims of a brain clot affecting his memory, have circulated widely. However, his wife Andrea Hewitt has dismissed these as false, stating he is fine. While Kambli has been seen using a walking stick, his family asserts he is recovering.

title: Jacqueline Fernandez seeks to turn approver in Rs 200 crore Sukesh Chandrashekhar case
content: Bollywood actress Jacqueline Fernandez has filed a plea to become an approver in the Rs 200 crore money-laundering case involving Sukesh Chandrashekhar. The Enforcement Directorate is investigating the alleged extortion racket. Fernandez, who has been questioned previously, has now expressed her willingness to cooperate with the agency by turning approver in the high-profile case.

title: Rohit Shetty house firing: UP STF & Mumbai Police arrest 9th accused in Agra - Report
content: In a significant development, Pradeep Kumar, also known as Gaath, was apprehended in Agra by the Uttar Pradesh STF in collaboration with the Mumbai Police Crime Branch. Kumar has been identified as a pivotal member of the Shubham Lonkar gang, implicated in the shooting incident at filmmaker Rohit Shetty's residence in Mumbai. Read on to know more in detail.

title: How drones deployed from aircraft, helicopters could change aerial warfare
content: India's Air Force is enhancing its offensive capabilities by seeking drones deployable from helicopters and loitering munitions from transport aircraft. These advanced systems, capable of various missions including electronic warfare and target acquisition, aim to free up fighter jets and counter adversaries effectively. Similar initiatives are underway globally, highlighting a significant shift in future warfare strategies.

title: India's longest range electric scooter: Simple Ultra launched with 400 km range!
content: Bengaluru-based Simple Energy has launched the Simple Ultra, India's longest-range electric scooter with a claimed 400 km IDC range. Priced at Rs 2.35 lakh, the scooter boasts a 6.5 kWh battery, a top speed of 115 kmph, and a 0-40 kmph acceleration of 2.77 seconds.

title: Shredded to cosmic dust: Stunning footage captures comet’s death plunge into the Sun — watch
content: Indian space enthusiasts, brace yourselves! Nasa spacecraft have witnessed the dramatic demise of comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) as it ventured too close to the Sun. Observations from SOHO, STEREO, and PUNCH revealed the icy wanderer disintegrating into a dust cloud, likely hours before its closest solar approach.

title: Army Commander's conference concludes, lessons from ongoing conflicts discussed
content: In a landmark gathering, India's foremost military leaders came together for a three-day Army Commander's Conference dedicated to transforming the armed forces into a cutting-edge 'Future Ready Force'. The agenda was rich with strategic discussions on integrating advanced technologies, achieving robust networking capabilities by 2026, advancing unmanned systems, and extracting valuable insights from current global conflicts.

title: 2027 recruiting class rankings: Oklahoma among top 10 schools off to strong start
content: more than 60% of the players in the SC Next 300 remain uncommitted . the summer months will be pivotal in shaping the rankings . eight teams that finished with a top-10 class for the 2026 cycle have picked up where they left off with classes again near the top nationally .

title: 19 going on 35: How Konnor Griffin's maturity is making an impression on Pirates

title: Delimitation explained: Since 1951, Hindi belt’s Lok Sabha share has fallen more than South's
content: between 1951 and 1977, both the Hindi belt and the southern states saw their share of Lok Sabha seats decline . the main reason was not a gain for the South over the North . but the growing representation of Union Territories and the rising share of western and eastern states .
